mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal ongoing economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If in a rolling 2-year window, months with positive payroll growth are followed by negative 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 8% from elevated post-recession levels, it confirms a durable labor market recovery, boosting confidence and corporate profits, and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE triggers, the average forward 60-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or positive return rate < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 2%) produce negative real interest rates, which reduce the discount rate on equities and push investors out of cash, driving S&P 500 prices higher.
failure_condition: If less than 60% of triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS month-over-month > 0.5%) signals robust consumer spending, a key driver of GDP, leading to immediate positive investor sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: If after trigger, the 5-day forward return is negative more than 60% of the time, or mean return is not statistically > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 600,000 units per year indicate recovery in the housing sector, which supports residential investment, employment, and household wealth, lifting equities over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 600 but forward 60-day returns are not positive in at least 70% of observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing and output, which typically leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If months with positive INDPRO change have a forward 20-day return distribution not significantly above zero (rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter > 2%) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE month-over-month decline) confirms a self-sustaining expansion, prompting upward earnings revisions and equity gains over the ensuing quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q change > 2% and UNRATE declines from prior quarter
failure_condition: If the joint condition fires but the 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over all occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2%) and near-zero interest rates (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%), policymakers maintain accommodation, growth expectations are supported, and equities deliver positive returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but the 60-day forward return is not above the sample median in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
